name: macOS Host Overview (Conduit default)
description: Default host metrics + system logs collected by Conduit's darwin profile. Every metric panel is broken down by host.name so multi-host fleets split out automatically; use the Host preset filter at the top to scope to a single machine. Uses percent-form *.utilization metrics (cpu, memory, filesystem) and the parsed log fields (severity_text, process, normalized_message) emitted by the agent.

panels.content:
# macOS Host Overview

Default platform telemetry from **Conduit's darwin profile** ([`internal/profiles/darwin/`](https://github.com/conduit-obs/conduit-agent/tree/main/internal/profiles/darwin)), with every panel broken down by `host.name` for multi-host fleets.

| Section | Source | What it shows |
|---|---|---|
| Load | `metrics` | `system.cpu.load_average.{1m,5m,15m}` (gauge) |
| CPU | `metrics` | `system.cpu.utilization` (0..1 fraction) by `state`, idle filtered out |
| Memory | `metrics` | `system.memory.utilization` (0..1 fraction) by `state` (`used` / `inactive` / `free`) |
| Filesystem | `metrics` | `system.filesystem.utilization` (0..1 fraction) for `/` and `/System/Volumes/Data` (the operationally meaningful APFS volumes) |
| Disk | `metrics` | `system.disk.io` (cumulative sum, auto-INCREASE) by `device` + `direction` |
| Network | `metrics` | `system.network.io` (cumulative sum) restricted to `enX` interfaces; `system.network.connections` by TCP state |
| Logs | `prod-infra` | Volume by `severity_text` and template counts via the parsed `process` + `normalized_message` columns |

**Filter** by `Host` to scope all panels to one machine. **Filter** by `Service` if you run multiple Conduit services per host (rare).

**Reading the utilization charts**: values are 0..1 fractions. A `system.memory.utilization` of `0.82` means 82% of memory is in the queried `state` (e.g. `used`).

_Dashboard generated by Conduit V0 MVP. Stored as a checked-in definition under `dashboards/` in the Conduit repo._

panels.name: Load Average (1m / 5m / 15m) by host
panels.description: Three-window load average from system.cpu.load_average.{1m,5m,15m}. Sustained values above core-count = overloaded host.

panels.name: CPU Utilization (% non-idle) by state, per host
panels.description: AVG of system.cpu.utilization (0..1 fraction) by host.name + state, with state=idle filtered out so the chart shows only user / system / interrupt — i.e. effective CPU load.

panels.name: Memory Utilization by state, per host
panels.description: AVG of system.memory.utilization (0..1 fraction) by host.name + state. On darwin the states are used (active + wired), inactive (page-cache reclaimable), and free.

panels.name: Filesystem Utilization (% used) by mount, per host
panels.description: MAX of system.filesystem.utilization (0..1 fraction) restricted to / and /System/Volumes/Data — the two operationally meaningful APFS volumes. The other ~10 synthetic volumes share the same underlying storage and just clutter the chart.

panels.name: Disk I/O by Device + Direction (per host)
panels.description: SUM of system.disk.io broken down by host.name + device + direction. Honeycomb auto-applies INCREASE to the cumulative counter so values are bytes-per-bucket.

panels.name: Network Throughput by Interface (per host)
panels.description: SUM of system.network.io broken down by host.name + device + direction, restricted to enX interfaces. macOS internal interfaces (anpi*, awdl0, bridge0) are filtered out for signal-to-noise.

panels.name: TCP Connections by State (per host)
panels.description: AVG of system.network.connections by state. ESTABLISHED + LISTEN dominate on a healthy box; growing CLOSE_WAIT is usually a leak in user code.

panels.name: Log Volume by Severity (per host)
panels.description: COUNT of prod-infra events broken down by host.name + severity_text. Filelog-parsed lines arrive with severity_text=INFO by default (set by Conduit's transform/logs processor); upstream OTLP logs and journald entries keep whatever severity their source set.

panels.name: Top Log Templates (per host)
panels.description: COUNT of prod-infra events grouped by host.name + process + normalized_message, top 25. Uses the masked normalized_message column (UUIDs, IPs, key=value values, and 4+ digit numbers replaced with *), so each row is a distinct log template rather than a unique line.
